steps.title: Make two quick data rows
steps.content: Use a table instead of just translating the phrase. If $x=0$, then $y$ is $49$ more than twice $0$, so $y=49$. If $x=1$, then $y$ is $49$ more than twice $1$, so $y=51$.

steps.title: Fit the line in Desmos
steps.content: Run $y_1 \sim mx_1+b$. Desmos gives $m=2$ and $b=49$, so the equation is $y=2x+49$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice A matches the fitted equation. Choice B swaps the roles of $2$ and $49$, choice C incorrectly combines them into $51x+2$, and choice D uses the wrong slope. Choice A is correct.
